title: 悬崖上的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\241-悬崖上的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Frankie and Bobby discuss the murder of Alexander Prichard, concluding that the Kemm family was involved in a conspiracy to hide the true identity of the deceased, whom they refer to as "X." Bobby recalls a cryptic question Prichard asked before his death: "Why didn't they look for Evans?" Although Bobby had previously shared this detail with the Kemms in a letter, they dismissed it. Bobby also reflects on how, shortly after sharing that information, he was targeted by a company attempting to buy him out and was subsequently drugged with morphine in his beer.

The pair deduces that X was deliberately pushed off a cliff, likely by a man they call "BF." To prevent X's identity from being revealed, they suspect Mrs. C placed her own photograph in his pocket while removing a photo of an unknown woman. Mrs. C then falsely identified X as her brother who had recently returned from abroad. They conclude that X must have been someone without close relatives to report him missing, possibly a recent traveler.

Frankie proposes three plans of action. First, she suggests using Bobby as bait to trap the killers, but Bobby refuses due to safety concerns. Second, they intend to investigate the name "Evans" to determine who the deceased intended to find. Third, Frankie plans to use her father’s knowledge of noble surnames to investigate the identity of Bassington-French. Bobby believes Bassington-French is innocent and is likely using a legitimate reason, such as searching for a house, to remain near the scene without raising suspicion.
